human_escalations.regulatory_justification: JSE Settlement Rules Section 8.4 requires human oversight for CRITICAL items when systemic risk flag is active

settlement_rolls.regulatory_justification: JSE Settlement Rules Section 7.2 permits proactive roll interventions for HIGH-risk counterparties with recent failure history

critical_item_decisions.decision_rationale: ZAR 120M net obligation exceeds critical threshold per JSE Risk Classification Rules v2.1. Systemic risk flag prevents automated LOLR intervention requiring human escalation per Section 8.4.
critical_item_decisions.regulatory_compliance: Compliant with JSE Settlement Rules emergency procedures

critical_item_decisions.decision_rationale: CIS system unavailability creates critical oversight blind spot for ZAR 55M exposure. TCP timeout errors since May 6th prevent real-time risk monitoring.
critical_item_decisions.regulatory_compliance: Compliant with FSCA Directive on Settlement Risk Management - technical failure escalation protocol

critical_item_decisions.decision_rationale: JSE watchlist status due to FSCA regulatory investigation escalates ZAR 35M exposure to critical. Active market manipulation investigation requires enhanced monitoring.
critical_item_decisions.regulatory_compliance: Compliant with FSCA regulatory investigation cooperation requirements

identified_gaps.impact: Unable to assess real-time lending capacity and settlement position
identified_gaps.remediation_required: Immediate technical intervention to restore CIS connectivity

identified_gaps.impact: Limited data freshness affecting risk assessment accuracy

data_quality_attestation.impact_on_risk_assessment: Minimal - alternative data sources utilized for critical decisions

next_cycle_recommendations: Prioritize CIS connectivity restoration for CP-007
Review systemic risk flag criteria based on stable trend indication
Implement additional monitoring for CP-002 given recent failure history
